# macs2_atacseq_callpeak

## What This Does
Call ATAC-seq accessibility peaks with MACS2/3 tuned flags.

## Use This Skill When
- Use for ATAC-seq peak calling to identify open chromatin regions.
- Capability: atacseq_analysis
- Analysis category: atacseq_analysis

## Do Not Use This Skill When
- Not for ChIP-seq (use macs2_chipseq_callpeak) or RNA-seq analysis.

## Required Inputs
- `genome_size` (string, required): Effective genome size (hs, mm, etc.).
- `name` (string, required): Output sample label.
- `output_dir` (path, required): Output directory.
- `treatment_bam` (path, required): ATAC paired-end BAM file.

## Input Types
- bam

## Expected Outputs
- bed
- tsv

## Tools And Requirements
- macs2
- `min_cores`: 2
- `min_ram_gb`: 8

## Execution Contract
The harness executes the typed contract in `contract.yaml`. Do not invent file paths, skip required inputs, or bypass the wrapper.

Wrapper command template:

```text
macs2 callpeak -t {treatment_bam} -f BAMPE -g {genome_size} -n {name} --outdir {output_dir} --nomodel --shift -100 --extsize 200
```

## Validation Notes
The harness should verify required outputs exist and match the declared artifact roles before downstream steps use them.

## Source Notes

The source contract includes these notes:

Use for ATAC-seq peak calling with paired-end fragment mode.

## Onboarding Metadata
- Source: https://macs3-project.github.io/MACS/docs/callpeak.html
- Source Mode: official_docs
- Installed At: curated_seed_v1
